prompt: Say: I am reading a short story now.
correctAnswer: Estoy leyendo un cuento corto ahora.
hints: Practise this phrase out loud.

prompt: Say: Yesterday I studied for two hours.
correctAnswer: Ayer estudié durante dos horas.
hints: Practise this phrase out loud.

prompt: Say: Every morning I write three new words.
correctAnswer: Cada mañana escribo tres palabras nuevas.
hints: Practise this phrase out loud.

prompt: to be (permanent qualities)
correctAnswer: ser
hints: Use 'ser' for nationality, profession, and permanent characteristics.

prompt: Say: She is a teacher.
correctAnswer: Ella es profesora.
hints: No article before profession with 'ser'.

prompt: Which verb means 'to have'?
correctAnswer: tener
hints: Not 'ser' or 'estar'.

prompt: Say: The books are on the table.
correctAnswer: Los libros están sobre la mesa.
hints: Use 'estar' for location.

prompt: to be (location / temporary state)
correctAnswer: estar
hints: Use 'estar' for where something is and how someone feels right now.

prompt: Say: They are from Argentina.
correctAnswer: Son de Argentina.
hints: 'Ser + de' expresses origin.

prompt: Say: I play football on Sundays.
correctAnswer: Juego al fútbol los domingos.
hints: 'Jugar a + sport'; present tense yo = -o ending.

prompt: Say: Tomorrow I will practice with my friend.
correctAnswer: Mañana practicaré con mi amigo.
hints: Pay attention to the sentence structure.

prompt: Say: If I have time, I will review the lesson.
correctAnswer: Si tengo tiempo, repasaré la lección.
hints: Pay attention to the sentence structure.

prompt: Say: We were cooking when the guests arrived.
correctAnswer: Estábamos cocinando cuando llegaron los invitados.
hints: Pay attention to the sentence structure.

prompt: imperfect tense
correctAnswer: pretérito imperfecto
hints: Used for past habits, descriptions, and interrupted actions.

prompt: Say: I used to play the piano when I was young.
correctAnswer: Tocaba el piano cuando era joven.
hints: Imperfect for past habits: tocaba, era.

prompt: Choose the correct form for a past habit: 'She ___ early every day.'
correctAnswer: se despertaba
hints: Imperfect for repeated past actions.

prompt: Say: Spanish is easier than I expected.
correctAnswer: El español es más fácil de lo que esperaba.
hints: Comparative: 'más + adjective + de lo que'.

prompt: Fill in: ___ (I was eating) cuando sonó el teléfono.
correctAnswer: Comía
hints: Imperfect for a background action interrupted by another event.

prompt: Say: I get up at seven every morning.
correctAnswer: Me levanto a las siete cada mañana.
hints: Reflexive verb: 'levantarse'; yo form = me levanto.

prompt: Say: He arrived before we left.
correctAnswer: Él llegó antes de que nos fuéramos.
hints: 'Antes de que' triggers subjunctive.

prompt: Say: I have been studying Spanish for six months.
correctAnswer: He estado estudiando español durante seis meses.
hints: Notice the tense or modal used.

prompt: Say: I would travel more if flights were cheaper.
correctAnswer: Viajaría más si los vuelos fueran más baratos.
hints: Notice the tense or modal used.

prompt: Say: I had already left when the message arrived.
correctAnswer: Ya me había ido cuando llegó el mensaje.
hints: Notice the tense or modal used.

prompt: subjunctive mood
correctAnswer: modo subjuntivo
hints: Used after expressions of wish, doubt, emotion, or impersonal phrases.

prompt: Say: It is necessary that you arrive on time.
correctAnswer: Es necesario que llegues a tiempo.
hints: Impersonal expression + que + subjunctive.

prompt: Choose the correct form: 'I hope she ___ (come).'
correctAnswer: venga
hints: 'Esperar que' triggers subjunctive.

prompt: Say: The book was written by a famous author.
correctAnswer: El libro fue escrito por un autor famoso.
hints: Passive: 'ser + past participle + por'.

prompt: Fill in: Dudo que él ___ (know) la respuesta.
correctAnswer: sepa
hints: 'Dudar que' + present subjunctive.

prompt: Say: She has never been to South America.
correctAnswer: Ella nunca ha estado en Sudamérica.
hints: Present perfect with 'nunca'; 'nunca' goes before the verb.

prompt: Say: They asked me to help them move.
correctAnswer: Me pidieron que les ayudara a mudarse.
hints: 'Pedir que' + imperfect subjunctive.

prompt: Say: By the time we arrive, they will have finished dinner.
correctAnswer: Para cuando lleguemos, ellos habrán terminado de cenar.
hints: Focus on natural phrasing and word order.

prompt: Say: Had I known earlier, I would have changed the plan.
correctAnswer: Si lo hubiera sabido antes, habría cambiado el plan.
hints: Focus on natural phrasing and word order.

prompt: Say: If I had practiced more, I would be more confident now.
correctAnswer: Si hubiera practicado más, ahora tendría más confianza.
hints: Focus on natural phrasing and word order.

prompt: nominalization
correctAnswer: nominalización
hints: Using an infinitive or adjective as a noun: 'el hablar', 'lo importante'.

prompt: Say: Whatever you decide, I will support you.
correctAnswer: Sea lo que sea que decidas, te apoyaré.
hints: Concessive with double subjunctive: 'sea lo que sea'.

prompt: Which sentence is a contrary-to-fact present condition?
correctAnswer: Si tuviera tiempo, estudiaría más.
hints: Second conditional uses imperfect subjunctive + conditional.
options: Si tuve tiempo, habría estudiado más.

prompt: Say: Speaking fluently requires consistent practice.
correctAnswer: Hablar con fluidez requiere práctica constante.
hints: Infinitive as subject of the sentence.

prompt: Fill in: Buscaban a alguien que ___ (speak) tres idiomas.
correctAnswer: hablara
hints: Subjunctive in relative clause after an indefinite antecedent.

prompt: Say: The phenomenon we observed cannot be explained by current theory.
correctAnswer: El fenómeno que observamos no puede explicarse con la teoría actual.
hints: Passive 'se' construction with modal.

prompt: Say: It is as if he had never left.
correctAnswer: Es como si nunca se hubiera ido.
hints: 'Como si' + past perfect subjunctive for a contrary-to-fact comparison.
